device_name: Vantage Fortian/Orian 1.5T, MRT-1550, V9.0 with AiCE Reconstruction Processing Unit for MR
intended_use: Vantage Fortian/Orian 1.5T systems are indicated for use as a diagnostic imaging modality that produces cross-sectional transaxial, coronal, sagittal, and oblique images that display anatomic structur
intended_use_full: Vantage Fortian/Orian 1.5T systems are indicated for use as a diagnostic imaging modality that produces cross-sectional transaxial, coronal, sagittal, and oblique images that display anatomic structures of the head or body. Additionally, this system is capable of non-contrast enhanced imaging, such as MRA.
ground_truth: For PIQE: conventional method (matrix expansion with Fine Reconstruction and GA Filter) as reference; For NeuroLine+: manual annotation by two experienced ARRT licensed MR technologists; For Free Breathing Dynamic: manually selected phases by experienced radiologists; For IMC: motion-free acquisitions as reference
other_metrics: PIQE: reviewers exhibited strong agreement at 'good' and 'very good' level for all IQ metrics (SNR, sharpness, ringing, overall IQ, feature conspicuity); NeuroLine+: 97.5% success rate for auto-positioning (acceptance criterion >80%); Auto Scan Assist: resulted in less time and fewer steps compared to manual slice positioning; Free Breathing Dynamic: 90.9% arterial phase detection success rate (acceptance criterion ≥80%); IMC: significantly reduced motion artifacts with improved SNR, tissue contrast, sharpness, and diagnostic confidence
